title: packera paupercula var. pseudotomentosa
intro: Packera paupercula var. pseudotomentosa, also known as Western Yarrow, is a perennial plant species within the aster family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating wounds and skin irritations due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating menstrual cramps, and relieving respiratory issues such as colds and flu. Herbal preparations of Packera paupercula var. pseudotomentosa can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used externally to treat skin conditions or internally to boost immunity.
